string.text: Misriah Armory is an up and coming start up from a Australian backwater with the goal of designing weaponry that could eventually endure the trials of future space travel. At first laughed out as naive, their first prototypes already began a reputation of rugged, reliable if expensive weapon systems that's quickly raising calculating eyebrows from many state militaries.\n Their SP-5 (Special Project) Magnum Pistol is a heavy yet compact, semi-automatic, recoil-operated, 12-round magazine-fed, large-caliber handgun chambered for .50 AE. The weapon has a polymer handle, along with a titanium alloy body and an electroless nickel-plate finish, and a few test samples have been sent to interested parties, which is how you suspect it ended up in the Zone. Soldiers that tested it are on record stating they can't decide if this might be either the world’s biggest pistol or the world’s smallest rifle.\n
Titanium alloy made magazine that hold 12 .50 Action Express rounds for the SP-5 Magnum handgun.
